programmes.show_description: In 2011, a spate of unexplained hospital deaths in northern England triggers a police hunt for a serial killer, with evidence pointing to one man - Victorino Chua.

programmes.show_description: Paedophile Roy Whiting murdered eight-year-old Sarah Payne after being released from prison. Would he be caught if he hadn't made a series of errors before and afterwards?

programmes.show_name: **Visually Signed** Ben and Holly's Little Kingdom
programmes.show_description: The Wise Old Elf tells Holly and Ben that no magic should be used at school. Holly finds it difficult to get by until Nanny uses magic to help her. (VS)
Work and Play: When Peppa and Suzy learn that grown ups must work all day, they decide to play at working in a shop. But they quickly find it is very tiring!
The Rainbow: Peppa and her family spot a rainbow when they go for a drive.
Tweedy and Fluff find a ladybird in the craft room. Tweedy makes it a bed to sleep in but soon discovers that ladybirds aren't pets and they don't need cosy beds after all. (Ep12)
While playing hide and seek, Squiggle realises he can't count to five. With help from his friends, he learns by counting items around him and returns to resume the game with Fluff.
...Big Adventures! Brenda learns that it's important to work as a team and not just care about her own tasks.
It's the Annual Pontypandy Bread Baking Competition and Hannah 's ambition accidentally causes her to spark a wildfire.
Daddy Pig has a book from the library that he has kept for too long. The family go to the library to return it and find more books to read.
Paddington helps to hatch an egg but it takes ages & when it finally cracks open, he gets a real surprise!
When Pip and Posy team up for Frankie's dino egg hunt, Pip gets annoyed that Posy didn't find any of the eggs.
Big Hairy's little brother falls into a hole, and Tracker gets carried away by Big Hairy; Danny tries extreme rock climbing with Ace Sorenson.
...Proof Farm. Rubble and Motor come up with crafty construction solutions to fix Farmer Zoe's squirrel problem before the Farm Fair.
Granny and Grandpa Pig are taking Peppa and George to a festival - a jigsaw festival! Peppa is amazed by all of the completed jigsaws around the festival.
It's the day of Penny Polar Bear's party! The children find out they will be playing ice hockey! They are kitted out in safety gear at the ice rink and have great fun learning to play.
Tim tries to relax, but Tia has other ideas.
Grandad is in Gran's good books for tidying his desk, but he tells Stan that it wasn't him! Who are the mystery tidy-uppers and how did they get in?
When Farmer Al finds mysterious crop circles in his corn field, he calls on the Paw Patrol to find out who - or what - is making them.
When Milo, Lofty and Lark are enlisted to protect the Royal Cookbook from Crafty Mr Croc, they end up trying to save Agent Stone too!
Things are going missing all over the Little Kingdom, and soon Ben and Holly realise that ants are stealing their things!
When all the families have a boat race at the lake, Daddy Pig soon regrets picking a boat with pedals.
Odo tries to help his dad assemble a flat-pack table.
Olivia mixes colours and learns about solutions, while creating a swirling colourful pattern using a plate of sweets. (Ep5)
Join Olivia for a story about a day when Georgia notices objects moving around the house. Is it something spooky!? Clever Georgia debunks the mysteries with explanations and no fear!
Start the day with a dose of hard-hitting debate, your phone calls and a little fun - all driven by Jeremy's unique style.
Two of 5's much-loved faces team up to focus on the issues that matter most to viewers - with your opinions front and centre.
National and international news.
Matt looks at the big news stories developing during the day and offers help and advice to save you money and make your lives a little easier.
Raw, unfiltered, authentic and personal, Vanessa Feltz wants you to tell her what's on your mind. Fiery discussions, a good giggle and plenty of afternoon gossip!
After a murder takes place onboard during a presentation by a renowned wellness guru, Jack is on the case - and the prime suspect is a disgruntled patient.
Colin 'Coco' Brown is a drag queen by night and runs his own animal rescue centre by day. Today he's preparing to wave adios to two rescued ponies, Tony and Bunny.
Join Dan Walker and the team for the news that matters to you.
Pigeon racing has deep working-class roots, especially in industrial South Wales. We meet a father and son who demonstrate that pigeon racing is not just a sport but a way of life.
In Lyme Regis, Susan visits the historic Cobb to pay homage to a classic film, meets showbiz legend and Carry On star Anita Harris, and takes to the water with the Gig Boat racing team.
Jeremy Vine hosts as team captains Carol Vorderman and Sally Lindsay are joined by broadcaster Jason Mohammad and comedian Ed Byrne, hoping to bring their sharp minds to the table.

programmes.show_name: New: Building the Impossible Rob Bell
programmes.show_description: Rob travels the Panama Canal to learn how the seemingly unconquerable isthmus was bent to the will of determined engineers, creating one of seven wonders of the new world.

programmes.show_name: Mysteries of the Pyramids with Dara O Briain
programmes.show_description: With the Royal Pyramids of Egypt as a backdrop, Dara delves into why and how these structures were built, and explores their appeal to alternative theorists.

...Taking Down the Shoplifting Gangs. Police piece together incriminating clips to tackle suspects linked to a crime group. In Burnley, cops pursue prolific shoplifting suspects.

programmes.show_name: **Visually Signed** Ben and Holly's Little Kingdom
programmes.show_description: A mole disturbs King Thistle's golf course, so a gnome is enlisted to help drive it away. Soon the gnome is ordering the elves around. (VS)
When the Mixmups spy a rainbow in the Sky, they go on an adventure to find out what lies at the end of it. (Ep32)
Travel across the UK with the PAW Patrol, meeting real-life heroic hounds - from medical detection pups to mountain rescue dogs. These pups make a real difference to peoples' lives!
Peppa and George take a bin of vegetable peelings to Grandpa Pig's compost heap.
Madame Gazelle gets Peppa and her playgroup friends to dance to all different styles of music.
Holly goes to school for the first time. Ben comes along, despite protests from the Wise Old Elf.
While the trains are setting up for the Vicarstown Fall Festival, Thomas is feeling down about the end of summer and the changing weather.
Sam and Penny are called into action after Mandy gets trapped in some mountain caves while in search of a bat.
Tim fakes it to prove he's just as cool as Kai's cousin.
Grampy Rabbit's new jet pack shoots off into the distance and he ends up on top of a snowy mountain!
When Daddy's car goes missing it's up to Detective Potato to find it.
The friends are forming a band and Posy gives out roles to everyone except Mo, who feels left out. She must figure out how to properly include him.
The sandcastle which the Woohoos made yesterday has disappeared and so has the sea! They find out about tides and discover the sea can be far away from or close to the beach.
Paddington wins a raffle and a meal for two at Sofia's cafe, but must choose a best friend to go with him, either Jonathan, Judy, Mateo, or Mr Gruber.
Pigeonton has nested in a lamppost outside Mr Gruber's shop, but the council has condemned it, so Paddington decides to find him a new home.
Sid Swashbuckle the Pirate and his first pup mate Arrby steal the giant pearl-making shell that gives Puplantis its magic.
The Cadets spend the day at Harper Asprey Wildlife Sanctuary, where they help care for a rescued bat, look after a badger named Pedro, and lend a hand with a rescued jackdaw.
Sam and Kit arrive in the forest to figure out what's causing trees to go crashing towards the residents below. The search leads them to curious tunnels.
After failing his driver's test again, Mr Krabs decides to teach SpongeBob how to drive.
Patrick invents a new board game and invites SpongeBob, Sandy and Squidward to play it with him.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: In Dorset, Ben spends a week with Chris, a former paratrooper who has spent the last five years walking the UK's coastline, finding love and raising thousands for charity.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Ben returns to Western Australia to catch up with charismatic widow Barbara. Since he last saw her, there has been a serious cancer scare and a devastating bush fire...
Graeme Hall returns. A deaf Dalmatian is ruining his owner's love life. A Married at First Sight star is given the runaround by the other love in his life, his Bantam Bulldog.
A dog's loyalty to dad causes chaos at home, three troublesome pooches turn walks into a nightmare, and one dog's violent reaction to noise puts her life at risk. Can Graeme Hall help?
...Royals with Rev Richard Coles & Emily Andrews. The duo discuss whether Harry and Meghan should return to royal life to help repair the monarchy's reputation.
Peter treats a pet sheep who has an unpleasant case of flystrike, with maggots inside his ear. The Huddersfield team perform complicated spinal surgery on a French bulldog.
Violet enlists Clemmie's help to catch a runaway lamb. And Amanda takes the three youngest girls to the lambing pastures to teach them how to keep watch over the expectant flock.
Clive has a surprise for 12-year-old Edith, who will become the proud new owner of a sheepdog puppy in just a few days' time.
This week it's the summer holidays and 17-year-old Reuben has taken some time off to help his parents with their biggest summer job, hay timing.
National and international news.

programmes.show_name: To the Manor Born: Britain's Best Loved Comedy
programmes.show_description: Laugh-filled look at the hit sitcom starring Dame Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles, featuring a new interview with Dame Penelope, plus classic clips and archive.
...What Are They Really Saying? Our most skilled lip readers go beyond the headlines to reveal what they believe was said in the extraordinary days following the death of Elizabeth II.
...Groomsman: The Scandal That Nearly Brought Down the Empire. With fascinating new evidence, this programme unravels the whispered romance between the widowed Queen and her devoted servant.
Monty Python caused a storm with The Life of Brian, Margaret Thatcher became the UK's first female Prime Minister, and a David Bowie video stunned audiences. (Ep3)
